dimensions.note: Credential resolution stays delegated to the AWS SDK via env/profile (fromIni); no secrets are vaulted or logged. The new ssoSession argument is a session NAME (not a token); the pre-flight resolves the cached token through the shared lib's injected resolver and only its remediation message (no token bytes) is surfaced. SHARED_RETRY/preflightSso/ssoRemediation come from the SDK-bearing _lib/aws_credentials.ts twin, generated only into this producer (the report bundle stays SDK-free).

New pre-flight branch logs at warn with structured placeholders ({session}, {message}) for both the expired short-circuit and the proceed-on-network case, mirroring the default-sg tracer. The completion info log is emitted on both the short-circuit return and the normal return. No credential/token values appear in any message.

classifyError now imported from the canonical _lib and checks `network` BEFORE auth_expired (locked by a new unit test: a getaddrinfo failure wrapped in a CredentialsProviderError classifies network, not auth_expired). Per-(profile,region) phases still throw nothing — every failure becomes a scan_error and the sweep continues. The expired SSO pre-flight short-circuits with one actionable error; a transient network pre-flight deliberately does NOT short-circuit (a re-login would not fix a DNS blip). Each scan_error now carries the failing `service` (rds/sts/sso/'').

Smoke suite gains an expired-pre-flight short-circuit case (one sso-tagged scan_error, zero instances swept) and a transient-network pre-flight case (sweep proceeds). Unit suite gains a network-before-auth_expired classifyError case and an sts-tagged credentials-failure assertion. The pinned key smoke tests are rewritten to the canonical 4-arg single-hyphen/ambient/account format with a new distinct-keys-via-discriminated-phase assertion; the malformed-row key-uniqueness tests assert the folded `:ordinal:rowId` phase tail. Success and failure paths both covered; runSweep is driven through createModelTestContext and an in-memory AwsApi replay.

Sweep remains read-only (Describe* + GetCallerIdentity) and re-runnable; rows are keyed deterministically so a re-sweep overwrites in place. A single bad account/region/row still degrades to a scan_error rather than aborting. The SSO pre-flight is resolve-only (no mutation) and an empty ssoSession preserves today's behavior exactly.

No AWS request/response mapping changed. SDK client retry config moved from the local CLIENT_RETRY {maxAttempts:5} to the shared SHARED_RETRY {retryMode:'adaptive', maxAttempts:3} spread into both the RDS and STS clients — adaptive mode adds client-side rate limiting so sustained throttling backs off. The orthogonal page-level withRetry/paginate backoff (a separate concern) and the STS GetCallerIdentity withRetry wrap are KEPT unchanged. SDK pins unchanged at 3.1073.0.

Per-client safeDestroy() in the finally chains is unchanged; the pre-flight opens no SDK client of its own (token resolution is delegated to the injected resolver / shared-ini loader). No new file handles or network clients introduced by this change.

Manifest and model.version aligned at 2026.06.26.1. README updated for the new network kind, the service tag, the ssoSession pre-flight, and the canonical scan_error key (the stale `error--<profile>--<region>--<phase>` description is replaced with the single-hyphen ambient/account form). Test fixtures use fictional placeholders (orders-db, prod-readonly, ACCT_*); the one 12-digit literal in the instance/reserved key test is the documented self-delimiting-account-id contract.

MECHANICAL Schema-Write Conformance: scan_error now uses the canonical ScanErrorSchema (profile, accountId, region, service:default(''), phase, kind incl `network`, message, scannedAt) — strict z.object, no passthrough. Every writeError call site builds all 8 fields with a `service` tag; the canonical scanErrorKey is built from (profile, region, service, phase). instance/reserved schemas unchanged and still strict. No placeholder-laundering: the validate* gates still reject coverage-critical-missing rows before a record is written.
instance/reserved/scan_error all keep lifetime 'infinite' and garbageCollection 10 — unchanged.
Read-only fan-out sweep, not a CRUD lifecycle model; no create/update/delete surface to complete. Unchanged by this slice.

The new SSO pre-flight is an opt-in (ssoSession non-empty) check that runs once before the per-profile loop, with an injected resolver seam so the expired/ok/network branches are unit-testable without disk/SDK. It is read-only (token resolve), so it adds no mutation gate; the existing requiredProfileSuffix refusal still fires before any AWS call.

MECHANICAL Instance-Name review: scan_error is written only by writeError. The two per-row malformed phases fold a `:ordinal:rowId` discriminator into the `phase` segment, so the canonical key (which includes service+phase) is distinct per malformed row — verified by the same-region distinct-keys tests (hyphenated ids, reserved rows, and the id-less ordinal-fallback worst case). instance/reserved keys (instanceKey/reservedKey, `--`-joined) are unchanged and collision-free across account/region/id.

The sweep only writeResource()s; it does not readResource. No data-access pattern changed; the companion report's data dependency on instance/reserved/scan_error is preserved (scan_error gains additive `service`/`network`, read tolerantly downstream).

model.version bumped 2026.06.26.0 -> 2026.06.26.1 with a matching no-op upgrade entry appended (toVersion 2026.06.26.1, upgradeAttributes identity) so the chain tail equals model.version (verified by inspection; unlike its sibling producers this extension has no dedicated upgrade-chain metadata test yet — tracked as a follow-up). swamp upgrades transform stored globalArguments only; the new scan_error `service` field defaults to '' on reads so pre-existing stored rows still parse with no data migration.
